reviews.review_description:
I tried it seriously, didn't listen to the bad reviews, just wanted to have fun shooting, but unfortunately the game is completely immature. The embarrassing drivel, the really stupid opponents and the monotonous gameplay leave you bored after a short time.
I'm a huge Zero Caliber fan, but unfortunately what's delivered here is nothing compelling. I was looking forward to an After the Fall clone without zombies or at least a motivating campaign, but unfortunately it doesn't come close to After the Fall and a real campaign doesn't actually exist.
I hope Zero Caliber gets more content now that the multiplayer is finally outstanding and the campaign in coop is an absolute masterpiece. Unfortunately, unfortunately, unfortunately, I have to agree: Gambit! should be avoided. After all, the weapon handling is extremely good.
Only the co-op mode manages to salvage things somewhat, making it genuinely fun to just mindlessly blast everything away with your buddies.

Edit: The co-op/multiplayer no longer works. That makes the game pointless. I bought it exclusively so I could play with my buddies. I can't believe that the most important part of the game can just vanish like that. This is already the third game to undergo this treatment. It is unacceptable! I want my money back.
multiplayer didnt even work. and i couldnt even refund it. Waste of money. and a complete waste of time

I bought this game because i enjoyed the gameplay but knowing that the devs abandoned this game makes me wish that meta would allow us to refund the game even after the 2 hour limit to tell the devs if you won't finish your game then don't try making one. Now if this was an early access title i might be more simpathetic to the devs but ive never seen this game on early access. Meta could easily let us refund this game and have the money go into the meta cash but i don't see this ever happining

There's been no updates for over a year. The game was just ok to begin with. I believe they took down the discord page also. Get either or both of the Zero Caliber games. X Real did a great job with both of those games.

So if ur looking for a campaign, then pass. This is just single map horde modes disguised as levels. The writing is painfully unfunny, and the AI is terrible. 

It's a shame bc there is potential here. They should have made what they advertised. A borderlands knockoff, instead it plays like some crappy bonus mode in a bad call of duty game.

I want to preface this by saying I got this game for around $2 with a discount code. That will; without question, greatly influence what I say throughout this review.

That said... that this might be the best two bucks I've ever spent on a VR game.

Some of what I've read implies that there are two complete chapters. That is false: as of September 16, 2024 this game is one chapter long. This game is indeed unfinished. While the developer has promised two additional chapters for free, it's been over a year and they have not yet delivered (if they do, I will amend this review).

Within that one chapter, there's three levels that'll clock about 3~4 hours of playtime... and the moment you get to a point where you might find out more about the plot, the exposition dump they tease you with is postponed... because one of the main characters has to take a 💩 (no I'm not joking).

Also the online is dead. I've tried during "peak hours" and got nothing in both deathmatch and co-op campaign. There is a chance you can get luckier than I, but don't bank on it - if you wanna party up and play online you're likely gonna have to find other Gambit owners and structure an online game.

If none of that has dissuaded you so far... now you get to hear me talk about why this may be the best $2 I've ever spent in VR. First off is a reality check... If you've been heavily invested in VR for some time... you know we've paid more for less. How many of us were told that the $20 purchase of Batman Arkham VR on PSVR would net us one hour of gameplay then we knocked it out in 45m?

While Gambit does indeed just have three levels within one chapter, it's worth noting that those three levels are fairly large. If you put effort into finding all the crates, shooting all the cameras, looting the "Bet Bot" and locating the (admittedly well-hidden) "Monke" figures, you can spend well over an hour per level.

And if you do humor this game's collect-a-thon, you'll find out that the notion of this game being "just two pistols for three levels" is false. You don't just get two pistols, you get a decent upgrade tree, and in my playthrough pursuing the fruits of that upgrade tree gets rewarding. You also get to unlock more weapons - which I also felt was rewarding considering their own process of farming moolah and making upgrades. You can really go from death after death to effortlessly mowing people down.

The levels are structured in a way that will feel extremely familar to Left4Dead players - wherein one given level is broken up into roughly four-to-six checkpoint concatenated levels... and they're decently lengthy. Likewise I do genuinely feel that the element of replayability was not lost in translation even if we look at this in terms of only the three levels present. You're not likely to find everything in any given level on the first playthrough.

From systematic and mechanical standpoints things are... fine. This game looks and runs well enough to push the narrative forward on good looking standalone games. The AI does its job well enough so that I've got adequate firepower support, and I'm not down long if I die. Controls and aiming are responsive without holding your hand too much. There's a profanity filter available if sophomoric edgy jokes aren't your thing... but overall I didn't feel like there was anything glaringly game-breaking from a mechanical standpoint. I also like that it's got both offline content and that its offline content ISN'T zombies (there's an exhausting amount of zombie shooters in VR). I do also want to highlight the Municipal Mayhem mode - which essentially simulates online play with bots. Initially I thought this would be lame - then I proceeded to play half a dozen matches (another whole dozen followed the second day). While YMMV, playing against bots was pretty fun for me and it helps put currency in your pockets for upgrades.

I fully sympathize with the frustration expressed by everyone who spent $20 on a dream of a game that never got finished but; all-in-all, that one single chapter of three levels with all their sublevels and secrets and decent upgrades makes for something I felt was absolutely worth $2.. yes, even in its supremely unfinished state. So if you can get it with a huge discount... there's some nice solo fun here to be had.

Dear XRealGames, this game is FUN but it's downfall is YOUR ignorance of what could have been such a gem, such a beauty, this could have been a VR Hit like Blade and Sorcery or Even a Lesser But still Wildly successful game like Contractors, but instead y'all forgot about it and let it rot, and to the devs, IF y'all manage to see this, please add the missing chapters, and slightly rework how death works(when out of team revives the dead spectate instead of you all insta loosing GTA style and have them come back after the next checkpoint is reached) simply doing this will MASSIVELY boost this game back Up(Will also Need Bug fixes and some sort of Big Shout-out to remind owners of it's existence), My one star is to YOU,  not the game, for deciding that you would rather make a second Zero Caliber Than update this Game.

For anyone sifting reviews for this game to see if it is worth it, while it is incomplete and a bit buggy I HIGHLY recommend getting it as it is FUN and it is a good challenge, even though it has been forgotten.

Edit: Xreal games deserves to rot in h3||, they literally made a post on a game they choose to ignore just so they can advertise a deal on their popular games.

Its still playable only the First One-third. Great Game but when Most of it is still "coming soon" i am not willed to give 5 Stars. If you Like Games Like Borderlands its still a must have 👍 so devs, pleased gimme more of Frank, Udo, Cody and Kevin 😜
I feel nauseous almost every time I play.. and the filter to help with this just makes it worse because all it does is obscure your vision causing MORE discomfort... writing this after throwing up👌

…for $20, this game is economically priced. 

With the caveat that some extremely annoying choices were made in the final product — the screeching “Yoooouuuuuuuuuuu!” SFX that starts every single level (whyyyyy???); the often cringe attempts at edgy humor — the overall gameplay is entertaining, fast-paced and graphically smooth. The guns are fun to shoot. And I nearly beat the game before I realized you could purchase more than just the pistols you start with. (I enjoy pogo-ing around enemies with the shotgun in VR.)

The solo campaign is on the shorter side (more coming soon, or so they say) and in that regard a little disappointing. But hey, at least it’s not another roguelike!

Considering I spend nearly $20 on a movie ticket these days, I would say Gambit provides a similar return on investment. IDK. Worth $20, especially if you catch it on sale.


3.5 out of 5

(Could be a 4.5 with a few more campaign levels and 30% less edgelord.)

I cant believe how much fun this game is and why don't we have more chapters..please finish for those of us still playing.

I have changed my good rating to shameful! How can you just abandon this game the way you have??? We purchase these games with the anticipation of more content. Especially when promised. Very disappointing the way this game was abandoned....

When I first loaded up the tutorial I wasn't sure how much I'd be playing Gambit, let alone being able to recommend it to our group. I played the first section and became intrigued by the solid gunplay and jumping mechanics. However, it wasn't until I played the multiplayer that I knew we would be spending way more time here than anticipated! 
When our group partied up, we were put in the subway lobby, where we kept finding mystery after mystery. We found hidden buttons, switches, pipes to climb, a door with a counter, hidden areas to uncover, and this wasn't even the first level! We spent the next hour climbing to a billboard and painting all of our tags on the canvas and launching grenades down below. 
We finally made it to the first level and cleared it, learning quickly that your team shares lives and reviving is essential. Just as we were about to leave, one of our group members shot a lock off of a cooler and a message of "2 more" remaining in this area showed. We were thrilled to find the remaining ones in a  treasure hunt style search. Again about to enter the safe room, and another friend found a hidden button, then another, and a lever. After hitting and flipping things, some kind of Flying Blimp with Shield Panels Appeared. Next thing we know, we are all trying to shoot it out of the sky. We did it, and cash started falling out of it! 
By the end, we were all excited to play again and were all surprised by the amount of fun we had. Only reason we went to do something else is because our gaming group has 5 members and one member was left listening to us have fun for awhile. Awesome co-op fun.

reviews.review_description:
Let me start off by saying that this game is A LOT of fun. Now the developers are still working out a lot of the kinks here and there so don’t expect a polished platinum product, but don’t pay attention to all these negative reviews. And no, I don’t have a store code, the developers aren’t paying me for this review…I wish…I’ll break the game down pros and cons style to give you an idea of what to expect, as well as some things I’d like to see in the future

Pros: This is a true arcade style shooter. It’s very fast paced, and the action doesn’t let up at all. If the music is still playing, you ain’t safe yet. The graphics are surprisingly very good and the guns are rendered amazingly. The characters have very dis functional, almost insane personalities to them which provides a lot of humor, and each of they’re special weapons can instantly swing the game in your favor (who ever came up with a defibrillator as a weapon needs a raise) If you’re one of those people like me who doesn’t think it’s the end of the world if you can’t use a gun stock, than the aiming curve won’t even bother you. I prefer free handing anyway. The levels are also FILLED with secrets and collectibles to find so you’re definitely going to stay busy

Cons: As of now, the game doesn’t have a very big selection of firearms, which was a bummer but I eventually got it fast. Hopefully they add a much larger armory in the future. The AI can be absolutely brutal sometimes, and they’re far too accurate for an arcade style shooter. I started up one level and the AI popped me four times in rapid succession before I could even properly aim. Your character is very fragile and the AI doesn’t drop enough health pick ups to balance out how stupid accurate they are sometimes

Things I would like to see: Definitely more health for the player, or an armor buff like a purchasable ballistic vest in between stages. Heavy weapons like grenade launchers and machine guns for when the stages really start to get crazy (an M249 or M240B would be a game changer). Different grenade types to add more variety like flash bangs or smoke. And you can’t have an arcade shooter with melee options. I feel each character should have their own special close quarters weapon (Kevin just SCREAMS brass knuckles)
Love the story and dialogue I just hope more game modes come out where you can fight just bots with your teammates or capture the flag or something butt overall sick game

reviews.review_title: Fun good game, could be great
reviews.review_description: So to start. The game is fun. (Yes the ai are on the short bus side of things.) but that’s okay. The overall theme of the game is good. It’s enjoyable and there is enough of the game to see what the end result will be. There are some bugs( I unlocked the stag mask and it’s not usable for some reason) there are some inaccessible areas. There’s some puzzles/features that seem to not do anything without an explanation as to when it might be unlocked in the future (I’m looking at you access denied door in the starting station). With a strong player base (for multiplayer/pvp) and some future updates this could be one of the greatest shooters on quest. For now though, it’s not bad. It’s 20 bucks and worth it. It could be a 40.00 game if it had more refining and content.

reviews.review_title: Overall this is a fun game
reviews.review_description:
It was a fun experience. Really liked the absurd humor in the campaign.

One of the highlights of the game was definitely the coop and the multiplayer it made it even more fun
It was great to find out some secrets & collectibles.

All in all i would def recommend this game to anyone who is looking for some fun. It may not be the longest game, and yea had some bugs, but the replayability is good.
